task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function calculates the trimmed mean correctly, but could be more readable.
trace: To solve this task, I first checked if the input list is empty or contains fewer than 3 elements, in which case I returned None to comply with the edge case requirement. Then, I sorted the list and calculated the indices of the minimum and maximum occurrences excluding them from the calculation. Finally, I computed the mean of the remaining values and returned it as a float. This approach ensures that the function is correct, concise, and handles an edge case where the input list has less than 3 elements.
